Colombian football club

Itagüí Leones

**Itagüí Leones Fútbol Club**, also known as **Itagüí Leones**, is a professional Colombian [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") team based in [Itagüí](/wiki/Itag%C3%BC%C3%AD "Itagüí") playing in the [Categoría Primera B](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B "Categoría Primera B") starting from 2019. They play their home games at the [Estadio Metropolitano Ciudad de Itagüí](/wiki/Estadio_Metropolitano_Ciudad_de_Itag%C3%BC%C3%AD "Estadio Metropolitano Ciudad de Itagüí").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Itag%C3%BC%C3%AD_Leones_F.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

Founded as *Deportivo Rionegro* in 1944, Leones Fútbol Club participated in all seasons of the [Categoría Primera B](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B "Categoría Primera B") from 1991 to 2017, when the team was runner-up of the tournament and was promoted to [Categoría Primera A](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A "Categoría Primera A") for the first time.

The team played for almost forty years in the amateur league. The team's first president was Libardo Sanchez and his first manager was Alfonso Piedrahita. Thanks to its performances and achievements in the 80s the club was admitted in the first edition of the [Categoría Primera B](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B "Categoría Primera B"), the second-tier of the professional Colombian league, played in 1991. In 1993, with the direction of manager Carlos Augusto Navarrete, Rionegro managed to be among the five best of the tournament. The team got its best performance in 2001, when they were runners-up under the direction of manager Orlando Restrepo.

In 2008 Deportivo Rionegro, with [René Higuita](/wiki/Ren%C3%A9_Higuita "René Higuita") as their captain and star and with Óscar Aristizábal as manager, managed to win the first tournament of the year, defeating [Unión Magdalena](/wiki/Uni%C3%B3n_Magdalena "Unión Magdalena") in the final, but in the second half failed to reach the final, so they had to fight for the title of the Primera B against [Real Cartagena](/wiki/Real_Cartagena "Real Cartagena"), losing on aggregate by 2-4. They played the promotion matches against [Envigado F.C.](/wiki/Envigado_F.C. "Envigado F.C."), but lost by a score of 3-1 on aggregate. In 2014 Deportivo Rionegro moved to [Bello](/wiki/Bello,_Antioquia "Bello, Antioquia") and changed its name to *Leones Fútbol Club*. The following year they moved to [Turbo](/wiki/Turbo,_Colombia "Turbo, Colombia") where they stayed for another year before moving back to the [Medellín](/wiki/Medell%C3%ADn "Medellín") metropolitan area in 2016, establishing themselves in [Itagüí](/wiki/Itag%C3%BC%C3%AD "Itagüí").

Leones ended as runners-up in the [2017 Primera B](/wiki/2017_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_B_season "2017 Categoría Primera B season"), winning the Torneo Finalización and then going on to lose the final to [Boyacá Chicó](/wiki/Boyac%C3%A1_Chic%C3%B3_F.C. "Boyacá Chicó F.C.") on penalty kicks. However, since they ended the season as the best team in the aggregate table, they earned promotion to the [Categoría Primera A](/wiki/Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A "Categoría Primera A") for the [2018 season](/wiki/2018_Categor%C3%ADa_Primera_A_season "2018 Categoría Primera A season"), the first time the team played in the first division. They were relegated back to Primera B at the end of the season, finishing in bottom place of the relegation table.
